title: crunchy german potato bowl
description: fermented, crunchy, midnight-craving german dish featuring apple cider vinegar, stone-bowl dolsot. a twist on classic potato bowls, with a tangy fermented slaw and crispy potatoes.

ingredients.name: apple cider vinegar fermented slaw (see below)
dolsot stone bowl
ingredients.notes: available at most asian grocery stores

instructions: preheat the dolsot stone bowl in a toaster oven at 220°C (425°F) for at least 30 minutes before cooking.
peel the potatoes and cut them into 1-inch cubes, making sure they are all roughly the same size.
in a large bowl, mix together the apple cider vinegar, sugar, caraway seeds, salt, and black pepper.
add the cubed potatoes to the bowl and toss to coat with the vinegar mixture.
heat 2 tbsp of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
add the onion and carrots to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are caramelized and crispy, about 30 minutes.
remove the skillet from the heat and stir in the apple cider vinegar fermented slaw.

to assemble the bowls, place some of the potato mixture on the bottom of the dolsot stone bowl, followed by a spoonful of the onion and carrot mixture, and ending with a dollop of sour cream and a sprinkle of fresh parsley.

to make the apple cider vinegar fermented slaw, simply mix 200g of shredded cabbage with 120ml of apple cider vinegar and let it sit at room temperature for at least 2 hours, or until it reaches your desired level of fermentation.
